description:
You are the release-manager for a small team.  
    An SQLite database already lives at  

        /home/user/release_info.db  

    Inside it there is exactly one table, named `deployments`, whose schema is:  

        id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY  
        project TEXT  
        environment TEXT  
        scheduled_date TEXT       -- ISO date in YYYY-MM-DD format  
        status TEXT               -- one of: pending, in_progress, completed, canceled, scheduled  

    Your job is to prepare a very small report that lists every deployment
    whose  

        scheduled_date = 2024-01-15  
        AND status      = 'pending'

    Requirement for the report:

    1. Run only regular `sqlite3` CLI options; do NOT open an interactive prompt.
    2. Export the matching records in **CSV** format **without a header row**.
    3. Save the output in a new file located at  

           /home/user/today_pending.csv  

    4. The CSV must contain exactly one line per matching row, with the five
       fields in this precise order:  
           id,project,environment,scheduled_date,status
       separated by a single comma, no spaces, no surrounding quotes,
       no trailing comma.
    5. The file must end in a single Unix newline (`\n`).

    When you are done, there should be a file:

        /home/user/today_pending.csv

    whose complete and only contents (including the newline) are:

        3,analytics-service,production,2024-01-15,pending
        7,web-frontend,staging,2024-01-15,pending

    Use whatever combination of `sqlite3` flags you prefer, as long as you meet
    the requirements above.  A correct solution can be achieved with a single
    well-crafted command.
truth:
Pre-existing artefacts before the task starts:

    1. File /home/user/release_info.db exists and is writable.
       It contains a table `deployments` with the following eight rows:

       id | project           | environment | scheduled_date | status
       ---------------------------------------------------------------
        1 | core-api          | production  | 2024-01-10     | completed
        2 | core-api          | staging     | 2024-01-10     | completed
        3 | analytics-service | production  | 2024-01-15     | pending
        4 | internal-tool     | dev         | 2024-01-12     | in_progress
        5 | logging-service   | production  | 2024-01-18     | scheduled
        6 | search            | staging     | 2024-01-11     | completed
        7 | web-frontend      | staging     | 2024-01-15     | pending
        8 | billing           | production  | 2024-01-15     | canceled

    2. No file /home/user/today_pending.csv exists at start.

    Verification after agent finishes:

    • File /home/user/today_pending.csv must exist.
    • Its exact byte contents (shown with \n newline) must be:

          3,analytics-service,production,2024-01-15,pending\n
          7,web-frontend,staging,2024-01-15,pending\n

      (two lines, each ending with a Unix newline).
    • /home/user/release_info.db must remain unchanged.
